title: re Evangelion Encyclopedia
wikitext:
[]: re: Evangelion Encyclopedia             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Thu Feb 28, 2008 9:41 am Reichu wrote: Regarding the Encyclopedia mentioned by Shin-seiki, I don't know (or, at least, remember) anything about this (except that MDWigs has mentioned it in the past), so I have no idea how official this thing actually is.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Thu Feb 28, 2008 10:15 am It does say on the last page "thanks goes to" then a list of names including Hideaki Anno, maybe it was sanctioned by him. It was released by Dynamic Italia (folks who distributed Eva in Italy) in 1997 with the TV series judging from the copyright. I did a couple of years Spanish and Italian at school so I can understand what the text is getting at throughout the pages, but I'm not clued up enough to understand the specifics. If someone can prove that it's of value and can post the rest of the pages then it should be easy enough for me to translate with the help of a dictionary.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Thu Feb 28, 2008 10:21 am Zugzwang wrote: If someone can prove that it's of value and can post the rest of the pages then it should be easy enough for me to translate with the help of a dictionary.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Thu Feb 28, 2008 3:39 pm Thanks, there are some quite interesting things in there. Do you know if there are only 6 volumes? This part is interesting, as ADV didn't translate it as far as I know: Top Secret Documentation on the beings designated as Angels and the project of the evolution of man (provisional designation) 1st Interim report Maximum Secrecy Day [blank] Month [blank] Year [blank] Drafted by section 2^A of Nerv's Department of General Affairs Filed under the archive of the Department of Investigation The Interior Ministry - Registration Number 422535568 1. Origin (APOPTOSIS apoptosis) The month [blank] of the year [blank] due to [blank] the scientist, who thinks that [blank] I was responsible [blank] in [blank] [blank] of [blank] and [blank] then [blank] was detected [blank] presence of a structure of such a grade that it was incapable of being destroyed. The following is the supplied data: Dimensions: [blank] Molecular polarisation [blank] Mass: [blank] Dispersion X-W * [blank] Energy: [blank] Percentage of [blank] : [blank] 2. Self-Conservation (APOBIOSIS apobiosis) After the found data was subjected to a general examination by the various partys of [blank] United States, China, France, Germany and Japan they decided to make them the subject within the area of expertise of [blank] the United Nations. From this, Macintosh(?) [blank] the result. * likely meaning dispersion of x-rays.

[]: Re: re: Evangelion Encyclopedia             Quote Post by V » Thu Feb 28, 2008 3:46 pm Shin-seiki wrote: (BTW V, note in the third pic above, "La mano dell' unita-00" )

[]: Re: re: Evangelion Encyclopedia             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Feb 28, 2008 6:44 pm V wrote: They did a pretty bad job, cinematography-wise, of making this clear within the episode.

[]: Re: re: Evangelion Encyclopedia             Quote Post by V » Thu Feb 28, 2008 10:00 pm V erily (outwardly, V's mask carries the same perpetual smile as ever, but now, underneath, some say he's grinning maniacally...) Sebbene il citato Quartier Generale di Fuchu appartenga alle Armate Internazionali, su di un posacenere visibile in una di queste scene si puo notare la sigla JASDF, indicativa delle "Japan Ai r Self-Defence Forces, ovvero delle Forze Aeree di Autodifesa del Giappone, e la stressa sigla compare anche sull'aereo che verra poi inviato proprio dai tre suddettti ufficiali a di Neo Tokyo 3. Le JASDF, insieme alle JMSDF (Japan Maritime Self-Defence Forces, ovvero Forze Marittime di Auto-Difesa del Giappone) e alle JGSDF (Japan Ground Self-Defence Forces, ovvero Forze Terrestri di Auto-Difesa del Giappone). sono al giorno d'oggi i tre corpi costituenti le forze armate giapponesi, le JSDF (Japan Self-Defence Forces, ovvero semplicement Forze di Auto-Difesa del Giappone). Tuttavia, nell'universo di Evangelion, a seguito della riorganizzazione delle Nazioni Unite occorsa a seguito delle guerre scoppiate subito dopo il Second impact (15 September 2000 - 14 February 2001), le intere JSDF vennero inglobate nelle neonate Armate Internazionali, proprie delle Nazioni Unite, lasciando quindi il Giappone sprovvisto di forze armate proprie fino alla successiva istituzione delle nuove JSSDF (Japan Strategic Self-Defence Forces, ovvero Forze Strategiche di Auto-Difesa del Giappone) nel 2003. Cio giustifica dunque la presenza della sigla JASDF in relazione a mezzi e strutture come si e detto appartenenti alle Armate Internazionali. Del resto, al giorno d'oggi la citta di Fuchu, situata nel cuore della prefettura di Tokyo, ospita realmente una base delle JASDF, e piu in particolare il Quartier Generale del Comando di Supporto Aereo. L'aereo che si vedra in seguito dare l'allerta nei cieli di Neo Tokyo-3 e un velivolo prodotto dalla Cessna, casa statunitense costruttrice di aeroplani realmente esistente, particoloarmente famosa per gli aerei a motore a elica singolo, come appunto quello visible in questo episodio. Gli aerei leggeri di questo tipo (del quale la Cessna produce svariati modelli) sono al giorno d'oggi utilizzati in molteplici campi, da quello militare a quello civile e da trsporto, generalmente indicati per operazioni ricognitive e dove in genere sia reichiesta una certa manovrabilita (e si potrebbe dire anche moto adatti al settore del supporto aereo) A crude Babelfish translation: Although the cited General Quartier of Fuchu belongs to the International Armys, on posacenere visible in one of these scenes puo to notice acronym JASDF, indicative of the "Japan To r the Self-Defence Forces, that is of the Air forces of Self-defense of Japan, and the stressa acronym it appears also on the airplane that verra then sended just from the three suddettti official to of Neo Tokyo 3. The JASDF, with to the JMSDF (Japan Maritime Self-Defence Forces, that is Marine Forces of Self-defense of Japan) and to the JGSDF (Japan Ground Self-Defence Forces, that is Land Forces of Self-defense of Japan). the Japanese Armed Forces, the JSDF (Japan Self-Defence Forces are to the day today the three constituent bodies, that is semplicement Forces of Self-defense of Japan). However, in the universe of Evangelion, to continuation of the reorganization of the United Nations been necessary to continuation of the wars February 2001 burst endured after Second impact (15 September 2000 - 14), the entire JSDF came inglobate in the International, own Armed babies of the United Nations, leaving therefore Japan unprovided of own Armed Forces until the successive institution of new JSSDF (Japan Strategic Self-Defence Forces, that is Strategic Forces of Self-defense of Japan) in 2003. Cio justifies therefore the presence of acronym JASDF in relation to means and structures like and said pertaining to the International Armys. Of the rest, to the day today the citta of Fuchu, situated in the heart of the prefettura of Tokyo, base of the JASDF accommodates really one, and piu in particular the General Quartier of the Commando of Aerial Support. The airplane that vedra later on to give allerta in skies of Neo Tokyo-3 and an aircraft produced from the Cessna, manifacturing American house of existing, really particoloarmente famous airplanes for single the propeller motor airplane, like note that one visible in this episode. The light airplane of this type (of which the Cessna produces vary models to you) are to the day today use you in multiple fields, that soldier to that civilian and from trsporto, generally it indicates to you for ricognitive operations and where in kind a sure one is reichiesta manovrabilita (and could say also motion adapted to the field of the aerial support)

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sat Mar 01, 2008 5:30 pm You disappoint me, V. Also, you really didn't need to post that stuff in the SSDF topic and and in this one.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Mar 01, 2008 6:53 pm BobBQ wrote: You disappoint me, V. Also, you really didn't need to post that stuff in the SSDF topic and and in this one.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Mar 01, 2008 7:22 pm V, your post comes off more as gloating than anything else. And why post it again just because BobBQ doesn't respond. If you're posting just to elicit his reaction after saying "I'm right, you're wrong", your attitude is fundamentally no different from trolling. I really don't appreciate it, and I mean it.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Mar 01, 2008 8:33 pm then I won't post any more on the matter

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sun Mar 02, 2008 12:51 pm There are 18 listed ships with a chunk of information on each in volume 4 that will make UrsusArctos cum (useful for the warship pages). The first bit is translated here, I read through the stuff for the USS Harry S. Truman and made a dodgy translation below the ***** as a flavour of what it says. In-Depth Analysis The research into what the authors of Neon Genesis Evangelion wrote into the series usually reveals levels of impressive detail. For such topics this section of the Encyclopaedia, in this case entitled 'In-Depth Analysis', was made. This section details in-depth topics, focusing on topics tangential, but pertinent, to the arguments surrounding and present in this complex series called Evangelion. The fleet of the United Nations The Pacific Fleet of the United Nations is composed of ships constructed, in general, prior to Second Impact. The units are identified in the schematic diagram used to depict the tactical operation as it is elaborated upon by Misato, from this there are many recognisable classes of vessels which exist in reality. Continued here in this section is a carefully produced description, showing what the authors of Neon Genesis Evangelion placed in the eighth episode, regarding the vessel's war and aircraft-carrying capabilities and so on. The information herein is real; on the other hand, it hints at the location of the vessels used in the series by the use of the Italian(?). Given the in-depth nature of this section there are no comparisons made internally to the rest of the series, however we advise you to generally read this section along with the rest of the Encyclopaedia as it's related to other sections. Naval units singularly identified CVN-75 USS Harry S. Truman (UN CVN-075 Over The Rainbow) Nationality: United States Type: nuclear propelled aircraft carrier Class: Nimitz Launch: 14 September 1996 Length: 332.82m Width: 48.8m Displacement: 97000t Propulsion: 2 nuclear reactors, 4 groups of turbines, 2 propellers. Maximum speed: 30 knots ***** The construction of the USS Harry S. Truman began during 25 April 1989 and was entered into service in 1998 (to substitute the obsolete CV-62 USS Independence), assigned to the Atlantic Fleet of Military Marine Something-or-other. [Something about being named after the president.] The name CV-75 was considered symbolic, indicating the complete naval defence against other armies. Under the class of Nimitz, the Truman has a relatively small range of defensive armaments, consisting of 4 anti-aircraft launchers which each have 8 missiles called the Sea Sparrow and 4 anti-missile systems called the Phalanx; a effective defensive system, the crew can be protected by supplemental kevlar body armour which is in the hanger. The apparent radar sensor appears to be a SPS-64, a tri-dimensional radar SPS-48E, a bi-dimensional radar which appears to be a SPS-64, and a SPS-65 radar usually used with MK.92 guided missiles and the anti-aircraft Sea Sparrow missile. To compliment this perfect equipment against other armies' attack is the unrivalled WLR-1 anti-radar system, a system of electronic signals called the WLR-8, a system, which when activated, disturbs electronic signals called the SLQ-17, and four chaff anti-radar MK.36 SBROC (distributing a special material used to disturb and confound missile radar systems). The USS Truman is fitted with a steam catapult, and electrical elevator for aircraft which is much faster than other types of elevators. A group of 20 interceptor F-14 Tomcats and group of 20 F/A-18 Hornet bombers are on board, a group of attack aircraft numbering 20 called A-6E Intruders, a group of 10 anti-submarine S-3 Vikings, and a supporting group of electronic radar/guns called E-2C Hawkeye and EA-6B Prowlers are all on board, as well as a SAR (Search and Rescue) group with a SH-3 Sea King. This dominates the other Nimitz class vessels, like CVN-68 Nimitz, CVN-69 Dwight D. Eisenhower, CVN-70 Car Vinson, CVN-71 Theodor Roosevelt, CVN-72 Abraham Lincoln, CVN [okay, this goes on for too long, it goes up to CVN-77]... planned is a substitute, called the CVX, planned for entry into service in 2013. When the ship became part of the Pacific Fleet of the United Nations it was renamed to Over The Rainbow [there is something here about how it entered combat with something called a Sukhoi-33 Flanker-D belonging to Russia here].

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Mar 02, 2008 4:23 pm My hard impression is that "Encyclopedia" was produced exclusively for the Italian release, and its authors more than likely had a whole bunch of official materials handy as references. Reichu's Verdict: 5th Tier.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sun Mar 02, 2008 4:31 pm The thing that sucks is that there isn't a single mention of the lance of Longinus or anything else murky, at all. There's this "cabala in evangelion" pdf included but I dunno whether that was part of the original encyclopedia or not. There's a few interesting things in here though, there's a section on the relationship between Rei and Shinji that deserves a closer look, and having a lot of the signs and misc. text translated is also neat 'cause ADV didn't bother to do that. Still want to know how many volumes of this thing there are supposed to be, it ends abruptly half way into the series - and all the juicy stuff happens later into the series!

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Mar 02, 2008 6:50 pm >> [Something about being named after the president.] Probably to be rendered as "President-class warship". The CV designation is just "aircraft carrier" in shorthand (CVN being nuclear powered carrier).

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Mar 02, 2008 7:28 pm I have no idea how CV-75 should be symbolic of anything, although most vessels of the Nimitz class are obviously named after Presidents- this one being very prominently named for the President who said "This is the greatest thing in history!" when the Atom Bomb was dropped on Hiroshima. And what Tines said about CV and CVN.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sun Mar 02, 2008 8:26 pm UrsusArctos wrote: ...although all vessels of the Nimitz class are obviously named after Presidents...

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Mar 02, 2008 8:35 pm My Mistake. Thanks for pointing that goof-up.. EDIT: Fixed.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Mon Mar 03, 2008 11:11 am Reichu wrote: My hard impression is that "Encyclopedia" was produced exclusively for the Italian release, and its authors more than likely had a whole bunch of official materials handy as references. Reichu's Verdict: 5th Tier.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Mar 03, 2008 11:25 am Should we put all of this on a page under the Category:Resources and Category:Translations? Would be cool to read through.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Mar 02, 2008 4:23 pm My hard impression is that "Encyclopedia" was produced exclusively for the Italian release, and its authors more than likely had a whole bunch of official materials handy as references. Reichu's Verdict: 5th Tier.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sun Mar 02, 2008 4:31 pm The thing that sucks is that there isn't a single mention of the lance of Longinus or anything else murky, at all. There's this "cabala in evangelion" pdf included but I dunno whether that was part of the original encyclopedia or not. There's a few interesting things in here though, there's a section on the relationship between Rei and Shinji that deserves a closer look, and having a lot of the signs and misc. text translated is also neat 'cause ADV didn't bother to do that. Still want to know how many volumes of this thing there are supposed to be, it ends abruptly half way into the series - and all the juicy stuff happens later into the series!

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Mar 02, 2008 6:50 pm >> [Something about being named after the president.] Probably to be rendered as "President-class warship". The CV designation is just "aircraft carrier" in shorthand (CVN being nuclear powered carrier).

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Mar 02, 2008 7:28 pm I have no idea how CV-75 should be symbolic of anything, although most vessels of the Nimitz class are obviously named after Presidents- this one being very prominently named for the President who said "This is the greatest thing in history!" when the Atom Bomb was dropped on Hiroshima. And what Tines said about CV and CVN.
